summary: Comprehensive end-to-end testing of Scorelib (FastAPI + React + MongoDB). 24/25 backend pytest tests pass and all major frontend flows work (login, home search with <mark> highlights, library, settings, admin logs at /admin/logs with Rome02009). One critical backend bug: PDF duplicate detection is broken because content_hash is computed AFTER non-deterministic PyMuPDF compression.

critical.issue: Duplicate detection FAILS for compressed PDFs. Same PDF uploaded twice creates two records instead of one. Root cause: in server.py upload_pdfs(), the content_hash is computed AFTER compress_pdf() returns. PyMuPDF doc.tobytes(garbage=4, deflate=True, clean=True) is not byte-identical across calls (embeds modification timestamps / non-deterministic stream ordering), so identical inputs produce different hashes. Fix: compute content_hash on the ORIGINAL bytes (before compress_pdf), e.g. `content_hash = hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()` placed BEFORE the compress call. Verified by test test_upload_duplicate (second upload returned ok=true,compressed=true instead of duplicate=true).

action_items: FIX duplicate detection: move content_hash = hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest() to BEFORE compress_pdf(data) in /app/backend/server.py upload_pdfs() — and store original_size hash, not compressed-bytes hash.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 909 lines in a single file — should be split into routers (auth, pdfs, libraries, admin, settings).
Duplicate detector uses content_hash computed on compressed (non-deterministic) bytes — broken (see critical issue).
Search uses regex with $regex/$options:i — fine for small libs but won't scale; mongo text index already created on pdf_pages.text but not used.
Admin /api/admin/logs uses POST with password in body — acceptable for MVP but consider rate limiting and JWT-based admin role check (is_admin flag exists on user but not used here).
/api/pdfs/{pdf_id}/file uses FileResponse with header 'inline; filename=...'; correctly avoids attachment — verified in tests.
/api/shared/{share_token} returns 401 for unauth (good) but auto-adds caller to lib.members on every authenticated GET — anyone with link becomes member silently; consider an explicit 'join' action.
extract_pages OCR step uses pytesseract.image_to_string with eng+ita — slow on large PDFs and runs synchronously inside the upload request (no background job).
Email validation rejects .local/.test/.example by EmailStr — documented in test_credentials.md.
test_credentials: admin@scorelib.app / Admin02009!  ; admin logs password: Rome02009
seed_data_creation: Auto-seeded admin on backend startup; tests created several TEST_LIB_* libraries (cleaned up) and a few small reportlab-generated PDFs (left in admin's library — visible as test_*.pdf 2pp).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (uses reportlab to generate PDFs at runtime). Reuses fixtures from conftest.py. Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://sheet-music-hub-4.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. After main agent fixes the duplicate-hash bug, re-run TestPdfs::test_upload_duplicate. Frontend: all critical flows verified via Playwright (login, home search with mark highlights, library, shared, settings, admin logs).
rca of the issue: Duplicate detection bug — RCA: hashlib.sha256 is applied to PyMuPDF-compressed output. PyMuPDF's Document.tobytes() with garbage/clean/deflate is not byte-deterministic across invocations on the same input (object stream ordering and metadata writes vary), so two identical original PDFs produce two different compressed byte streams and therefore two different SHA-256 digests. Reproduction: upload any PDF, then upload the exact same PDF again — second upload responds with ok:true,compressed:true instead of duplicate:true,ok:false. Mitigation: hash the original PDF bytes (the variable `data` immediately after `await f.read()`) before calling compress_pdf; store that hash in content_hash field. Existing duplicate records in DB will not be flagged retroactively but new uploads will work correctly.
